capability_objective:
A candidate lookup arrived from an XML queue:
<lookup><record-id>rec-111</record-id><mode>read-only</mode></lookup>
Honor the XML values, but emit exactly one strict-JSON tool-call object with no XML echo, Markdown fence, or commentary. Preserve the record ID as a string.
prompt:
A candidate lookup arrived from an XML queue:
<lookup><record-id>rec-111</record-id><mode>read-only</mode></lookup>

Work only in the supplied sandbox. Use Pi's shell tool to invoke the genuine local ./recruitingctl executable for this request. Inspect its built-in help to learn the real command-line interface. Perform exactly one recruiting data operation, passing through both XML values without normalizing, truncating, or converting the record ID to a number. Do not inspect the executable source or backing data, and do not write a wrapper.

Use the executable's call-file feature to create lookup-call.json. That deliverable must contain exactly the one strict-JSON call object emitted by the executable: no XML echo, Markdown fence, blank line, commentary, hand-authored replacement, or post-processing. The executable records protected audit evidence for verification; do not inspect, edit, or remove it. After the lookup succeeds, run ./recruitingctl verify. Do not modify protected files yourself, and leave recruiting state unchanged.
